step_by_step_thinking:
For a 9-year-old child presenting with an Ellis Class III fracture in tooth number 11 two days later, the initial step would involve taking X-rays to assess the extent of the injury and considering whether there has been any displacement or involvement of vital structures like nerves or blood vessels. Given the severity indicated by Ellis Class III, prompt evaluation through imaging is crucial before deciding on further steps involving pulpal therapy if needed.
\boxed{A} Based on the information given about the Ellis Class III fracture, the correct approach involves evaluating the damage thoroughly via radiographs to ensure no significant structural compromise exists. The next logical step following diagnosis might include pulpotomy if indications arise due to necrotic tissue exposure or severe inflammation, aligning best with Option A.
